page: Microsoft Azure D16as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The server features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D16as_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng single-core score of 3767.78 and high single-threaded compression and decompression speeds. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average tier, with a passmark CPU score of 33162.55 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 30076.66. Memory performance is strong, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score of 2797.39. Large-block memory read and write speeds are average, measuring 51.11 GB/sec and 34.21 GB/sec respectively.

This server is designed for general-purpose workloads that require a balance of compute and memory resources without the need for local storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for tasks such as web serving, database operations, and small-scale language model inference, where it achieves strong prompt processing and text generation speeds on small models. However, performance degrades significantly on larger models, with prompt processing on a 70B model falling into the bottom tier.

The server is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, database operations, and small-scale language model inference, though it exhibits performance limitations on larger model workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD processors and dedicated compute resources for reliable multi-workload execution.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
